stairs_info_description:
**Important Notes – Stair Calculations**

1. **Each step** is calculated assuming construction with blocks laid lengthwise, with gaps between steps filled with mortar.
All elements are included in the calculations — **including the mortar used to fill the gaps.**

2. The **total height of the stairs** will always be slightly shorter than the raw input height.
This is because the thickness of slabs and adhesive provided in the input is subtracted during calculations. As a result, once the final slab is installed, the stairs will reach the **exact intended finished height.**

3. The **first step is always slightly smaller than the others.**
This is because it is calculated from the **ground level**. When building stairs, slabs are not installed on the ground itself, but on the first step.
Each subsequent step receives slabs and adhesive both on itself and on the step below, ensuring that the **final overall height remains consistent**.

4. **Always measure stairs from the finished ground level** (e.g. top of the patio, deck, or other finished surface).
We provide the exact size of the front slab based on this finished level.
If the ground level is **not finished yet**, you can make the first front slab longer and partially bury it in the ground. This gives you adjustment flexibility in case the final ground level ends up lower than expected.

5. **"Buried"** indicates how many centimeters below ground level you should excavate to start building each row.
The mortar layer below the first block is **already included** in the calculation.

6. All **cutting tasks and tile installation dimensions** are approximate and based on the calculated cut lengths and slab surface area.

7. For **45-degree mitre cuts**, we provide the **full slab size.**
Remember to cut off the corner at a 45-degree angle, ensuring that the **longest edge matches the calculated dimension.**
For the second slab (from arm B), the cut must be made from the **opposite side.**

8. **Waste notation:**

• **Waste: Step…** — the cut retains one original factory edge.

• **Waste: Rem Step…** — the cut has **no original factory edges** remaining.
stairs_open_info_section:
**Open Stairs – Important Information**
These are **open stairs**, where the **sides are flush with the steps**
They are **not enclosed stairs**, where the **sides extend above the steps.**

**Side Slabs**
Side slabs are **NOT calculated automatically.**
You must measure them separately and add them to your project manually.
Sorry for the inconvenience.

**Sides Explanation**
• Left Side
• Right Side
• Back Side

These sides represent the **side and back walls** of the stairs.

You should select them **only if you want them to be calculated**, and they are counted **only** in the following cases:

• **Back Side**
Count the back side only if there is **no existing wall behind the stairs.**
Example:
• Stairs built on a patio → the back wall must be built → **Back Side should be selected**
• Stairs leading to a house → the house wall already exists → **Back Side should NOT be selected**

• **Left / Right Side**
Side slabs are counted **only for sides that are exposed.**
Example:
• If one side of the stairs is against a wall (e.g. a house wall), you only need to count **one side, not both.**
• If the stairs are built in a corner, you usually build the **steps and one side only** — the second side should be deselected.
stairs_ushape_info_section:
**Front Length – Arm A (Stairs)**
Remember that in these stairs, the **front length (Arm A)** decreases by the **step tread depth** on both sides (each Arm B side).
Make sure you provide **sufficient length** for Arm A.

If Arm A is set to the same length as Arms B, the **last step (platform)** will be:

• **Half a size shorter on the front side (Arm A) than sides (Arms B)**
kerbs_info_description:
**Mortar Height (cm)**
The **Mortar Height (cm)** shown in the **Hunch Configuration** images represents the amount of mortar **underneath** the edge / kerb / set.

The mortar applied to the **sides** of the kerb (**hunch**) is calculated based on the selected **Hunch Configuration** image.

**LHW – Length / Height / Width**
**LHW** stands for:

• **Length**
• **Height**
• **Width**
slab_info_description:
**Total Frame Area**
When you add a **frame**, it is included in the **total area calculation.**

Example:
If your area input is **20 m²** and you add frames with a total area of **3 m²**, then:

• **Slab area** = 17 m²
• **Frame area** = 3 m²

**Number of Cuts (Ilość Cięć)**
You should count **all cuts**, both:

• **Lengthwise**
• **Widthwise**

If a slab needs to be cut in **both directions** (for example, a corner piece), it must be counted as **two cuts.**
info_description:
**Calculators**

The **Calculators** tab contains a comprehensive set of tools designed to estimate almost any task related to garden construction. Each calculator is built with a strong focus on **accuracy and detail**.

Every calculator automatically calculates **estimated hours for a specific task** based on time entries from you and your team. This means the final estimate is based on the **average performance of your entire crew**, not just a single person.

You can also review **individual employee averages** in a dedicated tab within the **Company Panel**.

All calculators were created to provide the most accurate possible estimates for both:

• **Required materials**
• **Time needed to complete tasks**

If you notice any inaccuracies or unexpected results, please report them to us as soon as possible — your feedback helps us continuously improve the system.

lshape_info_text: L-Shape Stair Calculator for stairs built in a corner with two walls. Each step has two arms (A and B) forming an L-shape. Arms shrink with each step. No side overhangs or side slabs (walls on both inner sides). All measurements should be in centimeters.
lshape_logic_step_lshaped: Each step is L-shaped, built from the ground up
lshape_logic_no_side_overhangs: No side overhangs (walls on inner sides)
lshape_logic_concrete_fill: Concrete fill: L-shaped gap behind blocks, from ground to step height
lshape_arm_a_too_short: Arm A ({{length}}cm) is too short for {{count}} steps. Minimum required: {{required}}cm. Reduce total height or increase arm A length.
lshape_arm_b_too_short: Arm B ({{length}}cm) is too short for {{count}} steps. Minimum required: {{required}}cm. Reduce total height or increase arm B length.
lshape_step_height_adjusted: Step height adjusted from {{from}}cm to {{to}}cm to fit {{count}} equal steps.
standard_step_height_adjusted: Step height adjusted from {{from}}cm to {{to}}cm to fit {{count}} equal steps.
ushape_step_height_adjusted: Step height adjusted from {{from}}cm to {{to}}cm to fit {{count}} equal steps.
ushape_arm_a_too_short: Arm A ({{length}}cm) is too short for {{count}} steps. U-shape requires 2× tread (both sides). Minimum required: {{required}}cm. Reduce step tread or increase arm A length.
ushape_arm_b_too_short: Arm B ({{length}}cm) is too short for {{count}} steps. Minimum required: {{required}}cm. Reduce total height or increase arm B length.
ushape_info_text: U-Shape Stair Calculator for stairs with two flights on opposite sides. Arm A is in the middle (against the wall), arms B (left and right) form the U-shape. Each step reduces arm lengths. All measurements in centimeters.
ushape_logic_step_ushaped: Each step is U-shaped, built from the ground up
ushape_logic_wall_behind: Wall behind arm A (between the two B arms)
ushape_logic_two_b_arms: Two B arms (left and right) on both sides of the flight
ushape_logic_concrete_fill: Concrete fill: U-shaped gap behind blocks, from ground to step height

wall_config_segments_desc: Wall divided into segments with individual heights.
